_notes.purpose: Concept-level knowledge graph for the AU × Oxmiq curriculum. Sits one level finer than docs/kb/graph.json (which is module-level). Each concept node is a single learnable idea; prereq edges define a learning order across phases.
_notes.source: Concepts extracted from planning/source-material/ across all four content phases (Inference Engineering, Prompt Engineering, AI Agents, Capsule Power User) plus Week 1 foundations from curriculum-mindmap.md.
_notes.first_pass: This is v0.1 — intentionally not exhaustive. ~100 concepts cover the spine; per-lesson frontmatter (introduces: / depends_on:) will refine this over time.

schema.title: human-readable name
schema.phase: one of: foundations | inference | prompting | agents | capsule | capstone
schema.introduced_in: list of module IDs from docs/kb/graph.json where the concept is first or substantively taught
schema.also_used_in: list of module IDs where the concept is reinforced or applied (optional)
schema.prereqs: list of concept IDs that should come before this one
schema.numerical_anchors: optional list of {value, label, citation} for citable facts attached to this concept
schema.assessed_by: optional list of {file, problem_id, difficulty} pointing into the Problem Sets appendices

_notes.changelog: v0.2 (3 Jun 2026): closed the 10-item KB-enhancement backlog. Added 17 dangling concepts + 7 Capsule splits; backfilled sources across every concept from phase-default appendix list; introduced numerical_anchors and assessed_by fields and populated them from the fortified appendices; added cross-phase also_used_in edges so the Phase-1-vocab-made-operational spine becomes visible to the edge-graph renderer.

concepts.title: Shell & Linux fundamentals
concepts.description: Navigating a Unix-like filesystem, running commands, piping output, basic permissions. Baseline for everything that follows.

concepts.title: Git workflow
concepts.description: Branch, commit, push, PR. The collaboration substrate for every code artifact in the program.

concepts.title: How computers run AI — the three-layer mental model
concepts.description: Application → inference engine → hardware. The mental scaffolding the rest of the curriculum hangs off.

concepts.title: Inference vs. training
concepts.description: Training writes the weights; inference uses them. Different hardware, different cost shape, different failure modes.

concepts.title: Token
concepts.description: The unit the model processes — a word fragment, ~4 chars in English. Pricing, context windows, and latency are all measured in tokens.

concepts.title: Context window
concepts.description: Maximum input+output tokens a model can process per request. Drives architecture choices (RAG vs. stuffing, chaining vs. monolith).

concepts.title: Temperature & sampling
concepts.description: Sampling parameter controlling output randomness. T=0 is deterministic; T=0.7 is typical chat default.

concepts.title: Latency vs. throughput
concepts.description: Latency = time per request; throughput = requests per second. Optimizing one often hurts the other.

concepts.title: Transformer architecture
concepts.description: Stack of self-attention + feedforward layers. The architecture behind every modern LLM.

concepts.title: Self-attention mechanism
concepts.description: Each token attends to all previous tokens via Q×Kᵀ×V. The mechanism that makes transformers parallelizable in training.

concepts.title: KV cache
concepts.description: Cached key/value tensors from past tokens, so each new token only computes against fresh K/V. The reason streaming generation is fast.

concepts.title: FlashAttention
concepts.description: IO-aware attention algorithm that tiles the computation to keep work in SRAM. Major speedup over naive attention.

concepts.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
concepts.description: Architecture where only a subset of expert sub-networks activate per token. Lower compute-per-token at the cost of memory footprint.

concepts.title: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA)
concepts.description: Attention variant that compresses KV cache via low-rank projection. DeepSeek's contribution to making long-context affordable.

concepts.title: GPU memory hierarchy
concepts.description: Registers → SRAM → HBM → system RAM. The hierarchy that determines whether your kernel is compute-bound or memory-bound.

concepts.title: GPU generations (Pascal → Hopper → Blackwell)
concepts.description: Generational changes in tensor cores, memory bandwidth, NVLink. Why hardware choice changes the inference economics.

concepts.title: The CUDA → engine stack
concepts.description: CUDA kernels → cuDNN → framework (PyTorch) → inference engine (vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, SGLang).

concepts.title: Inference engines (vLLM / TensorRT-LLM / SGLang)
concepts.description: The runtime that actually serves the model. Implements batching, KV cache management, paged attention, scheduling.

concepts.title: Continuous (in-flight) batching
concepts.description: Inserting new requests into the batch as soon as space frees, instead of waiting for the full batch to finish. Throughput unlock.

concepts.title: Paged attention
concepts.description: vLLM's KV cache management: variable-length KVs allocated in fixed pages. Reduces memory fragmentation.

concepts.title: Quantization (FP16 / INT8 / INT4)
concepts.description: Trading numeric precision for memory and speed. Trade-off curve against output quality must be measured per task.

concepts.title: Speculative decoding
concepts.description: Small draft model proposes N tokens; large model verifies in parallel. Latency win when draft is right.

concepts.title: Tensor / pipeline / data parallelism
concepts.description: Three axes for splitting a model across multiple GPUs. Each has different communication costs and ideal workloads.

concepts.title: Prefill–decode disaggregation
concepts.description: Run prefill (compute-bound) on one GPU pool and decode (memory-bound) on another. Frontier serving pattern.

concepts.title: Inference cost & economics
concepts.description: Cost per million tokens, ~10×/year decline, shared vs. dedicated, reserved vs. spot. Price per *completed task* is the right unit.

concepts.title: Chat-completion API anatomy
concepts.description: Structure of a chat-completion request: messages array with role-tagged turns, model, sampling params.

concepts.title: System prompt
concepts.description: Whole-conversation instructions sent with role=system. Conventionally holds persona, rules, output schema; cached at scale.

concepts.title: User turn
concepts.description: Per-request payload sent with role=user. Holds the dynamic content; rules belong in the system prompt.

concepts.title: The three vagueness traps
concepts.description: Undefined audience, undefined format, undefined success criteria. The diagnostic checklist for every ‘the model isn’t giving me what I want’ complaint.

concepts.title: Specificity beats vagueness
concepts.description: A specific prompt narrows the model’s output distribution. The single highest-leverage fix in prompt engineering.

concepts.title: Instruction position in long prompts
concepts.description: Models attend more to start/end of long contexts. Instructions buried in the middle (‘lost in the middle’) suffer.

concepts.title: Role / persona prompting
concepts.description: Telling the model ‘you are X’ shifts the output distribution toward how X writes. Probability shift, not magic.

concepts.title: Delimiters / data separation
concepts.description: Wrapping user-supplied data in XML-style tags so the model treats it as content, not commands. First line of defense vs. prompt injection.

concepts.title: Prompt injection (direct & indirect)
concepts.description: An attacker embeds instructions in user-supplied data, hoping the model treats them as commands. Direct = user attacks; indirect = retrieved content attacks.

concepts.title: Structured output (JSON / schema)
concepts.description: Forcing the model to emit machine-parseable output. Reliability tiers: prose → schema-by-example → prefill.

concepts.title: Prefill (speaking for the model)
concepts.description: Supplying an assistant-role message yourself; the model continues from there. Most reliable structured-output technique.

concepts.title: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting
concepts.description: Asking the model to write reasoning steps before the final answer. Works because previously generated tokens are the model’s only working memory.

concepts.title: Structured scratchpad
concepts.description: CoT wrapped in tags (<scratchpad>...</scratchpad>) so downstream code can strip the reasoning and ship only the final answer.

concepts.title: Few-shot prompting
concepts.description: 2–5+ input/output examples in the prompt so the model infers the task. Especially good for classification, formatting, style mimicry.

concepts.title: Few-shot example selection
concepts.description: Cover edge cases, show variation, be label-consistent, place hardest example last. Examples *teach*; pick them deliberately.

concepts.title: Hallucination (the honest framing)
concepts.description: Default behavior of a language model operating on questions it can’t reliably answer. Three structural causes: confabulation, false-premise, mid-generation drift.

concepts.title: Abstention guard
concepts.description: Explicit permission to say ‘I don’t know.’ Defense against confabulation — the model needs the option.

concepts.title: False-premise resistance
concepts.description: Frame questions to allow premise rejection. Don’t ask ‘why did X do Y’ if X didn’t do Y — the model may invent reasons.

concepts.title: Citation / quoted-evidence grounding
concepts.description: Require the model to quote source sentences for every factual claim. Defense against mid-generation drift.

concepts.title: Composing a complex production prompt
concepts.description: Putting every Week 6 technique together for a real use case (PR review, contract review, ticket triage).

concepts.title: Prompt chaining
concepts.description: Decomposing a complex task into 2–N simpler prompts with a JSON handoff. Localizes failures; testable independently.

concepts.title: Chain handoff format
concepts.description: The structured data (usually JSON) one prompt emits and the next consumes. The contract between chain steps.

concepts.title: Tool use (structured output + driver loop)
concepts.description: Model emits a structured function-call description; your code executes the function and returns the result as a new turn. The mechanism that makes agents possible.

concepts.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
concepts.description: Retrieve relevant passages from a corpus, stuff into prompt, instruct the model to answer only from those passages. Source of truth moves from weights to context.

concepts.title: Prompt evaluations
concepts.description: Automated test suite for a prompt: (input, expected, pass-criterion) tuples with a tracked pass rate. Same discipline as unit tests.

concepts.title: The agent loop (ReAct)
concepts.description: Perceive → Plan → Act → Observe → Repeat. The structural pattern under every ‘agent’ — chained prompts with tool calls in a loop.

concepts.title: Four converging capabilities (why agents work now)
concepts.description: MoE (cheaper) + FlashAttention (faster) + tool standards (MCP) + serving infra. The story for why agents are deployable in 2025 and not 2020.

concepts.title: Tool manifest
concepts.description: Structured description of available tools the model can call: name, description, parameters, returns. The contract between agent and tools.

concepts.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
concepts.description: Open standard for tool manifests + tool-server endpoints that any LLM client can consume. Supersedes provider-specific tool formats.

concepts.title: Agent-to-agent protocols (A2A)
concepts.description: Standards for one agent invoking another as a tool. Building block for multi-agent systems.

concepts.title: Blast radius & sandboxing
concepts.description: What an agent can damage if it goes wrong. Defense: least privilege, sandboxed execution, dry-runs, human approval gates for irreversible actions.

concepts.title: Agent governance & observability
concepts.description: Trace every agent action; gate dangerous actions; log everything; review failures. The discipline that makes autonomous systems shippable.

concepts.title: Multi-agent orchestration
concepts.description: Multiple specialized agents with a router. Tradeoffs: composability vs. coordination overhead, drift in long-horizon flows.

concepts.title: Where value accrues (model / infra / app)
concepts.description: Strategic question: which layer captures the margin? Industry view of where AI businesses defend value.

concepts.title: Deployed agent case studies
concepts.description: Klarna (2.3M conversations), Google coding agents (25% of code). Patterns: routine → escalate → learn. Failure modes: drift, silent failures, hallucination at scale.

numerical_anchors.label: Klarna AI assistant in first month

concepts.title: Capsule architecture
concepts.description: Client → server → cloud. The two connection paths (WebRTC and direct SSH). Where each Phase 1 / Phase 2 concept lives in the platform.

concepts.title: Capsule installation & auth
concepts.description: Install CLI, run ‘capsule auth login’, browser flow via Azure B2C, token caching. Headless via CAPSULE_AUTH_TOKEN.

concepts.title: Capsule environments (prod / public / dev / demo)
concepts.description: Four environments, different B2C tenants, separate user registries. Switching env requires fresh auth.

numerical_anchors.label: prod / public / dev / demo (separate B2C tenants)

concepts.title: Fleet discovery & filtering
concepts.description: capsule list, filter by GPU / VRAM / vendor / availability. Two diagnostic facts: tenant + customer.

concepts.title: Connecting to machines (term / exec / code / cursor / claude)
concepts.description: Five entry points to a machine. Routing by config-tag vs unique-id. WebRTC vs --direct tradeoffs.

concepts.title: Files, storage & streaming
concepts.description: OneDrive mount (persistent, cross-machine), SCP (one-off), file passthrough (dotfiles), capsule stream (WebRTC desktop/app), capsule docker (GPU containers).

concepts.title: Capsule reliability & diagnostics
concepts.description: capsule cleanup, diagnostic sequences, known failure patterns + recovery, bug-report writing.

concepts.title: capsule benchmark
concepts.description: Benchmark command. Phase 1 vocab made operational: concurrency = batching, tp = tensor parallelism, quant = quantization.

concepts.title: Benchmark methodology (change one variable)
concepts.description: Scientific method applied to inference: concurrency sweep, quantization comparison, multi-GPU. Record + plot.

concepts.title: Interactive quality evaluation (capsule chat)
concepts.description: Structured probing: factual, math, code, refusal. Spot quantization regressions. Speed vs. quality tradeoff.

numerical_anchors.value: 4 probe classes: factual / math / code / refusal
numerical_anchors.label: minimum quality matrix

concepts.title: capsule schedule (long-running jobs)
concepts.description: Submit, monitor, cancel non-interactive jobs. The right tool for any benchmark > 30 min.

concepts.title: capsule mcp (AI assistant integration)
concepts.description: Expose Capsule operations as MCP tools an LLM can call. Direct application of Week 7 to the Capsule CLI.

concepts.title: Capstone integration
concepts.description: End-to-end project pulling Phase 1 (inference) + Phase 2 (prompting) + Phase 3 (agents) + Phase 4 (Capsule operation) into a single deliverable.

concepts.title: Capstone evaluation discipline
concepts.description: Every capstone ships with: a benchmark on the inference side, an eval suite on the prompt side, and a failure log.

concepts.title: Attention position bias (lost-in-the-middle)
concepts.description: Long-context models attend more strongly to the start and end of the context window. Instructions buried mid-prompt are reliably under-weighted; place them at boundaries.

concepts.title: Citation discipline
concepts.description: Operational rule: every factual claim must be followed by a quoted span from the source corpus. Stronger than 'cite if you can'; defends against mid-generation drift.

concepts.title: Constrained decoding (grammar / JSON schema)
concepts.description: Engine-side mask over the logit distribution so only tokens valid under a supplied grammar (regex, CFG, JSON schema) can be sampled. Hard guarantee, not a prompt suggestion.

concepts.title: Delimiter discipline
concepts.description: Treat XML-style tags as the contract between trusted instructions and untrusted data. Same wrapper for every untrusted span; reject unwrapped user input as a code smell.

concepts.title: Eval suite
concepts.description: Versioned collection of (input, expected, grader) tuples shipped alongside the prompt. The unit-test equivalent for prompt regressions.

concepts.title: Few-shot learning (in-context)
concepts.description: Treating the few-shot examples as the model's training set for that single request. No weight update; the demonstrations alter the next-token distribution.

concepts.title: In-context learning
concepts.description: The model adapts behaviour from patterns in the prompt alone. Emergent property of large transformers; the substrate beneath few-shot and chain-of-thought.

concepts.title: LLM-as-judge
concepts.description: Using a second LLM call as the grader for an eval set. Cheaper than human labelling, but inherits the judge model's biases; calibrate against a human gold set.

concepts.title: NVIDIA Container Toolkit
concepts.description: Runtime hook that exposes host GPUs into Docker containers. `--gpus all` / `--gpus '"device=0"'` only works because this is installed; `capsule docker` assumes it.

concepts.title: nvidia-smi
concepts.description: Canonical GPU status CLI. First command after every `capsule term`; the diagnostic floor for VRAM, temperature, driver version, running PIDs.

concepts.title: Prompt as code
concepts.description: Treat prompts as source: version-controlled, reviewed, tested, deployed via the same pipeline as application code. No more 'prompt drift via Slack DM'.

concepts.title: Prompt caching
concepts.description: Provider-side KV-cache reuse keyed on the prefix of a prompt. System prompts and few-shot blocks become near-free on the second call; reorder prompts so the stable prefix comes first.

concepts.title: Prompt clarity
concepts.description: The umbrella property: audience defined, format defined, success criterion defined. Failure to clear all three is the single most common prompt defect.

concepts.title: RAG trust boundary
concepts.description: Retrieved passages are untrusted user input by another name. Wrap them in delimiters, never let them carry instructions, gate the corpus to vetted sources.

concepts.title: Reasoning models (o-series / thinking models)
concepts.description: Models trained to emit a long internal scratchpad before the final answer. Higher latency, higher token cost, materially better on multi-step reasoning benchmarks.

concepts.title: Self-consistency
concepts.description: Sample N chains of thought at T>0, return the majority answer. Trades cost for accuracy; the textbook win on arithmetic-style tasks.

concepts.title: Temperature & determinism
concepts.description: T=0 is the closest you get to a reproducible call; even then, batching, KV-cache layout, and floating-point non-associativity can flip outputs. Treat 'deterministic' as 'low-variance', not 'pure-function'.

concepts.title: Capsule routing: config-tag vs unique-id
concepts.description: Two ways to address a machine: friendly config-tag (e.g. `nv-3060-04-1`) resolved at the server, or unique-id for an exact box. Tag is sticky across re-images; id is not.

concepts.title: Capsule transport: --direct SSH vs WebRTC
concepts.description: WebRTC (default) tunnels through the relay; --direct opens an SSH socket straight to the host. Direct is faster but requires inbound reachability; WebRTC always works.

concepts.title: capsule stream (WebRTC desktop / app)
concepts.description: Stream a full desktop or a single Windows app to the browser. Mouse/keyboard relayed over the same WebRTC channel; latency dominates the experience.

concepts.title: capsule docker (GPU containers)
concepts.description: Run a container on the remote box with GPUs already wired through. Skips the Container-Toolkit setup; image cache lives on the host, not the client.

concepts.title: OneDrive mount (persistent home)
concepts.description: Per-user OneDrive bind-mount that survives re-imaging and follows the user across machines. The right place for dotfiles, datasets, model weights you care about.

concepts.title: capsule cleanup
concepts.description: Tear down local session state (cached tokens, stale port-forwards, orphaned containers). First step in every 'capsule is acting weird' triage.

concepts.title: capsule bug-report writing
concepts.description: What the on-call engineer needs in the issue: tenant, customer, machine tag, exact CLI invocation, timestamps, output of `capsule diag`. Same template every time.
